mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal a contraction in a key cyclical sector, leading to negative wealth effects and reduced consumer spending. This foreshadows a broader economic slowdown, causing a de-rating of corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average is below its 12-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts peaked in early 2006 and have been in a significant downtrend.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated Federal Funds Rate maintained for an extended period (over 6 months) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance. This increases the cost of capital for firms and tightens credit conditions, compressing profit margins and leading to lower equity valuations over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and has not been changed in the prior 6 months.
evidence_refs: fact:The FOMC has held the target rate at 5.25% since June 2006.
failure_condition: A portfolio strategy going short on trigger events fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy on a risk-adjusted basis over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which supports aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth. This positive economic momentum translates into positive equity market returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth has remained positive, indicating continued economic expansion.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive (p > 0.05) over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals stagflationary pressure. This environment squeezes corporate profit margins and increases the likelihood of a hawkish monetary policy response, both of which are negative for equity prices.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL growth is positive while year-over-year INDPRO growth is negative.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation remains a concern for the FOMC while manufacturing has shown signs of slowing.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO growth is not a statistically significant negative predictor of 60-day forward returns in a multivariate regression model.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise or strong upward trend in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates resilient consumer spending, which constitutes the largest part of the economy. The market reacts quickly to this information, pricing in higher near-term corporate earnings and driving positive returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following the release of a top-quartile month-over-month RSAFS print is not statistically different from the average return following a bottom-quartile print.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low (below an estimated NAIRU of 4.5%) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy. This forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, weighing on equity valuations as discount rates remain high.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is near cyclical lows, a condition historically associated with eventual Fed tightening.
failure_condition: The specified trigger condition does not precede periods of underperformance for the S&P 500 relative to its long-term average 20-day return.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and industrial sector. This is a strong coincident indicator of broad economic health and corporate profitability, supporting positive equity market performance over the following month.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.05 for any continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A recent real GDP (GDPC1) print showing strong quarter-over-quarter growth confirms broad-based economic strength. This reduces near-term recession fears and causes analysts to revise earnings forecasts upward, providing a tailwind for equities over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the most comprehensive measure of economic activity.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, the average 60-day S&P 500 return in quarters following a trigger event is not statistically greater than the average return in non-triggered quarters.
